title: Dendrophthora enckeifolia
taxonomy: Dendrophthora enckeifolia belongs to the family Santalaceae, a group of plant species commonly known as mistletoes. This classification places Dendrophthora enckeifolia within the order Santalales. The family Santalaceae comprises about 1,200 species, most of which are hemiparasitic, meaning they obtain some of their nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Dendrophthora enckeifolia is native to Central and South America and has been found in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. The classification of Dendrophthora enckeifolia is further detailed at the genus and species levels within the botanical classification system.

intro: Dendrophthora enckeifolia, also known as the Encke's mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species belonging to the family Santalaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, boasting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions including fever, infections, and skin issues. Bioactive constituents of Dendrophthora enckeifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
